title: Israel unleashes unlimited firepower in Iran and Lebanon
subhed: The country launched an operation against Hezbollah in Lebanon on Monday, after the Iranian-allied armed group fired rockets at it. The Israeli military is now seriously engaged in a two-front war.

title: France's nuclear deterrence takes major step toward Europe
subhed: During Monday's speech at the Ile Longue naval base, Emmanuel Macron stressed cooperation with France's allies across Europe, unveiled a new doctrine of 'advanced deterrence' and announced an increase in the number of France's nuclear warheads.

title: Iran war reignites debate over France's role in the Middle East
subhed: With fears of escalation around Iran stirring debate just two weeks before the municipal elections, France's position in the region is under scrutiny by its political parties.
byline: By Mariama Darame, Alexandre Pedro, Olivier Pérou and Marie Pouzadoux

title: What does international law say about the Israeli-American attack on Iran and Tehran's retaliation?
subhed: Aggression, self-defense, preemptive strikes: The United States, Israel and Iran each justified their military actions, but legal experts question their legality.

title: France's Iranian artistic diaspora torn between anxiety and joy
subhed: Since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, artists in the diaspora have found themselves dreaming of better days, even as they fear a rising death toll.

byline: Interview by Piotr Smolar (Washington (United States) correspondent)

title: Photographer Robert Frank's love letter to Paris
subhed: During a stay in Paris in the autumn of 1949, Swiss photographer Robert Frank created an album of the French capital for his fiancée, Mary, who had remained in the United States. In around 80 freely arranged prints, he captured the city's streets, inhabitants and everyday objects. This notebook has now been published, revealing the emergence of a style that would later explode in 'The Americans,' the photographer's seminal work.

title: Iranian regime scrambles to survive after Khamenei's death
subhed: Amid scenes of celebration and mourning, the formation of an interim government and ongoing bombings, Iran has entered a period of profound uncertainty, where the Islamic Republic's very survival and the reshaping of its internal balance of power are at stake.
